consistency.class_level_alignment: Consistent. Level 20 features are high-tier class capstones.
consistency.naming_convention: Consistent. 'Superior Inspiration' is a standard naming convention for high-level class features in D&D 5e.
consistency.description_format: Consistent. Follows the standard format for describing a class feature's mechanical effect.

quality.clarity: High. The effect is straightforward and easy to understand.
quality.flavor: Moderate. It reinforces the concept of the Bard's innate talent by automatically replenishing their primary resource upon entering combat (rolling initiative).
quality.power_level: Appropriate for a 20th-level feature. It provides a significant, reliable resource boost at the start of combat, which is crucial at this level, but only triggers once per encounter setup.

mechanics.trigger: Rolling initiative while having zero uses of Bardic Inspiration remaining.
mechanics.effect: Regain one use of Bardic Inspiration.
mechanics.interaction: This is a passive, automatic restoration, not an action or bonus action. It ensures the Bard is never completely out of their core resource pool when combat begins, providing a reliable safety net or offensive opening.
mechanics.balance_check: Balanced. It only restores one use, preventing infinite bursts, and only works if they were completely depleted, rewarding proactive use of the resource throughout prior encounters.

llm_audit.archival_notes: The feature mechanically grants a single recharge of Bardic Inspiration upon rolling initiative if it was exhausted. This is a standard, well-designed capstone mechanic for the Bard class, ensuring relevance in the final tier of play.
